sections.title: Business Units
sections.content:
| Unit | Name (Local) | Description | Focus | Status |
|------|-------------|-------------|-------|--------|
| Steel Profiles | Stahlprofile | Custom precision steel profiles via hot/cold rolling and drawing | Division under analysis | core |
| Drive Technology | Antriebstechnik | Gear units, winches, direct drive motors, automation, rotary table systems | Industrial drive systems | core |
| Investment Casting | Feinguss | Precision investment casting | Aerospace, industrial, medical | core |
| Sand Casting & Forging | Sandguss & Schmiedetechnik | Sand casting and forging of metal components | Industrial components | core |

Production sites: Laucherthal (steel profiles, casting, forging), Herbertingen (drive technology). Global presence across 11 plants and 7 subsidiaries in Europe, North America, and Asia.

sections.title: Strategic Context
sections.content:
ZOLLERN's Steel Profiles division operates in a structurally declining German metal processing market. Revenue erosion in traditional segments (automotive, general mechanical engineering) collides with a rigid fixed-cost base: IG Metall labor contracts prevent rapid headcount adjustment, machine capacity sits 30-40% idle, and per-unit logistics costs rise as volumes shrink. The company has already completed a cost reduction program but cannot cut its way to profitability.

The strategic imperative is **capacity utilization through new market entry** — finding high-value application markets that absorb idle tonnage and improve margin mix. Family ownership (Hohenzollern-Sigmaringen + Merckle) provides financial runway but not indefinitely. The near-net-shape value proposition (reducing customer machining waste by 40-80% vs. machined bar stock) is the primary entry wedge for new markets.
